checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output text against the source document and found no evidence of fabricated content. All paragraphs in the JSON correspond directly to sections found in the original letter. The document title and image alt text are appropriate and follow the 'Acceptable Changes' guidelines.

All contact information was verified. The phone numbers (919) 733-3055, (919) 733-4622, and (919) 733-3801 are identical to the source. The email addresses terri.reichert@ncmail.net and katherine.johnson@ncmail.net match exactly. The mailing address 2408 Mail Service Center also matches the source header.
The output contains the content from both pages of the source PDF. No pages or major sections (header, body paragraphs, contact info, sign-offs, cc list) were omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core text content, including all policy details and contact information, is fully preserved. While the repetitive physical location footer ('Location: 325 North Salisbury Street...') appearing at the bottom of each page in the source is not included in the JSON output, this is considered a minor omission of boilerplate text and does not meet the threshold for a 'significant' omission as defined by the criteria (being much less than 25% of any section and functionally superseded by the mailing address in the header).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording, dates, and form numbers (e.g., DSS-5104, DSS-5104-a, FSCWS-36-06) match the source exactly. Punctuation and whitespace normalization, such as merging text across page breaks or adjusting spaces around email addresses, were done appropriately and do not alter the meaning.

checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the document for duplicated text blocks, such as repeated paragraphs or sections. The closing 'Sincerely,' appears twice in both the source and the output, which is correct as it precedes two separate signatures. The body text and list of CC recipients are unique. No content duplication was found.

The heading hierarchy in the output is inconsistent with accessibility standards mentioned in the instructions. There is no H1 heading provided in the sections list; the first heading ('North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services...') is assigned H2 level. This creates a skip in the hierarchy. Subsequent headings follow a logical H2 to H3 progression.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document starts with agency identification and a formal subject line.
checks.b_evidence_output: The first heading in the JSON is "level": 2, followed by H2 and H3, but no H1 is present in the document elements.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables. This check is not applicable, resulting in a pass.

The reading order follows the source document logically. The header information (Governor and Director names) is linearized correctly. On page 2, the side-by-side signature blocks are linearized as 'Sincerely' -> Signature -> Title for the first signer, followed by the same sequence for the second signer. This preserves the intended flow of the document.

The 'cc' section at the end of the document is a visual list of individuals and roles. In the output JSON, this list is rendered as a single paragraph element with newline characters (\n) separating the items, rather than being structured as a list element with individual list items. This loses the structural semantics of the list.
checks.b_evidence_source:
cc Sherry Bradsher
Children’s Program Representatives
Work First Representatives
Family Support and Child Welfare Services Team Leaders
Local Business Liaisons
Sarah Barham
Child Welfare Attorneys

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly identifies and links the email addresses mentioned in the contact section of the letter. No broken or fabricated links were observed.

checks.a_analysis: In the converted HTML, the signature images have been swapped relative to the names and titles they accompany. In the source PDF on page 2, Jo Ann Lamm's signature is positioned on the left side above her name and title, while Hank Bowers' signature is on the right above his. In the HTML output, which uses a single-column layout, the handwritten signature image for 'Hank Bowers' appears immediately before the text for 'Jo Ann Lamm, Chief...', and the signature image for 'Jo Ann Lamm' (appearing as white text on a black background) appears before 'Hank Bowers, Chief...'. This layout error incorrectly associates the signatures with the wrong individuals, altering the visual organization and meaning of the sign-off section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 of the PDF shows two side-by-side signature blocks. Jo Ann Lamm's signature is on the left above her name. Hank Bowers' signature is on the right above his name.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows: 'Sincerely, [Hank Bowers signature image] Jo Ann Lamm, Chief...' followed later by 'Sincerely, [Jo Ann Lamm signature image] Hank Bowers, Chief...'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature section at the end of the document, following the main body text.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF document consists entirely of text paragraphs, headers, and signature blocks; there are no tables present to evaluate.

All meaningful images from the source PDF, including the North Carolina state seal and the handwritten signatures, are present in the HTML output. While the signatures are misaligned with their respective names (a layout issue), the images themselves are rendered correctly.

There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML rendering. Paragraphs and contact information appear only once in the document flow. The repetition of headings in the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
